layman_headline: The market is fearful and no alt setup is clean enough to justify risking capital right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a 'Fear' state with Bitcoin dominance high at 55.9%, which means money is flowing out of altcoins and into Bitcoin or stablecoins. Every swing candidate on the watchlist has significant problems — some already ran up 20-50% and are overbought, others are in clear downtrends with bearish chart patterns, and none offer a clean risk/reward entry. The smartest move right now is to stay patient and wait for a better setup.
layman_action: Do nothing this cycle. Keep your cash ready. Better entries will come when the fear starts to lift and a coin shows a clean technical bounce with volume confirmation.
expert_reasoning: Systematic review of all 9 candidates reveals no actionable setup: DCR has RSI at 19.6 (deeply oversold, our highest-confidence signal type) but already bounced +24% in 7 days with a bearish chart bias of -0.7 and poor R/R of 1.0x — this is a dead-cat bounce, not an entry. DEXE is RSI 77 and +54% in 7 days — clearly overbought and extended. KITE has a -1.4 bearish chart bias with a Bearish Marubozu pattern. ETHFI has the best chart bias (+0.6) but terrible R/R of 0.11x with resistance at $0.40 right above current price. JTO is moderately interesting (chart bias +0.4, pullback entry) but RSI at 59 isn't oversold and R/R is only 1.26x. GRASS dropped -21% but shows a Bearish Engulfing pattern with -0.9 chart bias — catching a falling knife. BTC dominance at 55.9% further suppresses alt performance. The Fear & Greed improving trend (24.6 weekly avg → 28 today) suggests we may be approaching a bottom, but we need confirmation before deploying capital.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 55.9% continues to crush altcoin performance — any alt entry faces a headwind
Fear & Greed at 28 is improving but could easily reverse if BTC breaks below $60K support
No volume breakout signals detected on any candidate — our most reliable signal type (100% win rate) is absent
watch_list: BTC price action around $60,000 support — a break below would trigger further alt selling; a bounce could signal risk-on rotation
Fear & Greed index crossing above 35 — would confirm the improving trend and signal safer alt entries
DCR if it pulls back to $11-12 range with RSI still oversold — could become a legitimate bounce trade with better R/R
market_context: Bitcoin is consolidating near $62K with high dominance (55.9%) while the broader market sits in Fear territory at 28/100. Institutional tokenization momentum (BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan joining UK taskforce) is a bullish macro signal but hasn't translated to price action yet.
context_banner: High BTC dominance and fearful sentiment make this a patience-is-alpha environment — no clean alt setups warrant capital deployment today.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) with improving trend from 24.6 weekly avg — suggests we're approaching a bottom but not there yet. Historically 80% reliable.
DCR RSI at 19.6 is deeply oversold but the +24% 7-day bounce means the easy money has already been made. No other candidate has an actionable RSI reading.
No volume breakout detected on any candidate. This is our most reliable signal (100% win rate, +13.4% avg return) and its absence is a strong reason to stay out.
BTC bull market forecast for September and $188M whale movement suggest upcoming catalysts, but timing is weeks away — not actionable for swing entry today.
BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan joining UK tokenization taskforce is structurally bullish for crypto but a slow-burn catalyst, not an immediate price mover.
